BILIRAN, BOHOL, CALAYAN, CEBU, LUBANG, LUZON: Abra, Aurora, Bataan, Batangas, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Ilocos Norte, Isabela, Laguna, Pangasinan, Rizal, MINDANAO: Surigao, Zamboanga, MINDORO, PALAWAN, PANAY, ROMBLON, SAMAR, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Forests and thickets, sometimes on limestone. 50-1400m.

species.citation: Ding Hou , Blumea 12 (1963) 35 ; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1964) 413.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN.

species.citation: Merr. , J. Straits Branch Roy. Asiat. Soc. 86 (1920) 325 ; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1964) 418.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. CEBU.
species.notes: Known from the Philippines from Bicknell 153, L. (?-)600m.

species.citation: Korth. , Verh. Nat. Gesch. Ned. Bezitt., Bot. (1842) 182 ; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1964) 415. = Salacia integrifolia Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 85 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 487.
species.distribution: Java, Moluccas, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. LUZON: Abra, Albay, Bataan, Ilocos Norte, La Union, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Surigao, Surigao del Norte, MINDORO, PANAY, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: Ding Hou , Blumea 12 (1963) 34 ; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1964) 417. = Hiptage lawsonii Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2751.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LEYTE, LUZON: Zambales, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Forests and in sandy gravelly ground along wooded banks. 0-225m.

species.citation: Wight , Ill. Ind. Bot. (Madras) 1 (1840) 134 ; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1964) 414.
species.distribution: Borneo, India, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Isabela, Nueva Ecija, Rizal.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 23 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 487; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1964) 411.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CEBU, LEYTE, LUZON: Benguet, MINDANAO: Surigao.

species.citation: ; Voucher: Gaerlan & Fernando, Philippine Plant Inventory # 19010 (L0824559), Dinagat, Campayas to Kaantojan - might represent this genus in the Philippines, but we have not yet confirmed its identification.

Griff. , Calcutta J. Nat. Hist. 4 (1844) 247 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 485; Ding Hou, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 394. = Siphonodon celastrineus var. acuminatissimus Merr. , EPFP 2 (1923) 485, as 'acuminatissima'; Type: BS 16335 Reillo (syn); Elmer 7144 (syn). = Siphonodon celastrineus var. subglobosus (Elmer) Merr. , EPFP 2 (1923) 485, as 'subglobosa'; ≡ Xanthophyllum subglobosum Elmer , LPB 5 (1913) 1677. = Siphonodon pyriformis Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 240 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 485; Type: Elmer 5985 (US, lecto). = Siphonodon pyriformis var. parvifolius Merr. , PJS 27 (1925) 33 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 252; Type: Loher 12809, Luzon: Rizal.
species.distribution: India, Myanmar, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam. BALABAC, BASILAN, CAMIGUIN DE BABUYANES, CATANDUANES, CEBU, LEYTE, LUBANG, LUZON: Abra, Albay (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Cavite, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, Laguna, NCR, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Tarlac, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon (photos), Cotabato, Surigao, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAUI, PALAWAN, PANAY (photos), POLILLO, SAMAR, SIARGAO, TABLAS, TICAO, TUBABAO.
species.notes: Throughout Malesia. Forests, sometimes in thickets. 0-1500m.
